scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a site-aware allocation recommendation with explicit capacity assumptions, demand drivers, and next actions.
Missing sites, channels, and systems-of-record produced a blocked-state prerequisite request instead of unsupported schedule advice.
Policy-exception approval for no-show fees stayed outside the lane and was packaged for the correct owner.
The booking and payment mismatch was summarized with clear source-of-truth boundaries and payment-owner routing.
Food-safety and chargeback-risk signals correctly stopped routine service handling and triggered evidence-preserving escalation.
Cross-lane work became a clean guest-services and front-office handoff with explicit unresolved items.
Capacity shortfall was quantified and turned into a policy-aware prioritization package rather than an implicit overtime or pricing override.
Tradeoffs that required discount, booking-cap, or overtime approval were routed outward instead of being decided in-lane.
High-volume queue handling stayed segmented by channel, site capacity, and record readiness instead of collapsing into generic support behavior.
Food-service terminology such as covers, turns, 86 list, BEO, comps, and voids was normalized into the correct artifact and decision frame.
Conflicting surcharge, no-show, and franchise policy signals were escalated instead of being synthesized into unsupported authority.
Tenant-specific service labels and queue names were adapted without losing baseline reservation, payment, capacity, or escalation controls.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
Functionality coverage is complete and this package remains a validated spec pack with specification-based runtime.
